name: SNMP Availability & Latency
description: Monitors SNMP reachability and response time. Provides availability tracking with configurable latency alerts for warning and critical thresholds.
author: MonCTL
changelog: v1.0.0 - Initial release: SNMP availability check via connector, three alert definitions (unreachable, high latency warning, critical latency)

apps.name: snmp_availability
apps.description: Measures SNMP reachability and response time via the built-in SNMP connector. Writes to availability_latency table for availability charts and alerting.

oid.title: SNMP OID
oid.description: OID to query for availability check

alert_definitions.name: SNMP Unreachable
alert_definitions.description: Device has not responded to SNMP for the configured window. Indicates device is down, SNMP service is stopped, or credential/firewall issues.

alert_definitions.name: High SNMP Latency
alert_definitions.description: Average SNMP response time exceeds warning threshold. May indicate network congestion, overloaded device, or routing issues.

threshold_defaults.description: Warning threshold for average SNMP round-trip time

alert_definitions.name: Critical SNMP Latency
alert_definitions.description: Average SNMP response time exceeds critical threshold. Device may be severely degraded or close to timing out.
alert_definitions.message_template: {device_name} SNMP latency is critically high at {value}ms (threshold: {threshold}ms)

threshold_defaults.description: Critical threshold for average SNMP round-trip time

versions.source_code:
"""SNMP availability & latency — measures SNMP reachability and response time.

Uses the built-in SNMP connector (bound as "snmp" on the assignment) for all
protocol operations.  The connector handles authentication (v1/v2c/v3) and
transport — this app only contains the monitoring logic.

Config keys (set on the assignment):
    oid       str   OID to query (default: sysUpTime.0 = 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0)

Connector binding required:
    alias "snmp" → SNMP connector with credential providing auth parameters

Writes to: availability_latency table
    rtt_ms          SNMP round-trip time in milliseconds
    response_time_ms  Same as rtt_ms (for compatibility)
    reachable       1 if SNMP responded, 0 if not
    state           0=OK, 2=CRITICAL
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import time

from monctl_collector.jobs.models import PollContext, PollResult
from monctl_collector.polling.base import BasePoller


class Poller(BasePoller):
    """SNMP availability poller — uses the SNMP connector for protocol ops."""

    async def poll(self, context: PollContext) -> PollResult:
        poll_start = time.monotonic()
        host = context.device_host or context.parameters.get("host", "localhost")
        oid = context.parameters.get("oid", "1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0")

        # ── Require SNMP connector ────────────────────────────────────────
        snmp = context.connectors.get("snmp")
        if snmp is None:
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=None,
                status="critical",
                reachable=False,
                error_message=(
                    "No SNMP connector bound (expected alias 'snmp'). "
                    "Bind the built-in 'snmp' connector with alias 'snmp' "
                    "on this app assignment."
                ),
                execution_time_ms=0,
                error_category="config",
            )

        # ── SNMP GET ────────────────────────────────────────────────
        snmp_start = time.monotonic()
        try:
            result = await snmp.get([oid])
            rtt_ms = round((time.monotonic() - snmp_start) * 1000, 2)
            execution_ms = int((time.monotonic() - poll_start) * 1000)

            value = result.get(oid, "(no value)")

            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[
                    {"name": "snmp_reachable", "value": 1.0,
                     "labels": {"host": host, "oid": oid}},
                    {"name": "snmp_rtt_ms", "value": rtt_ms,
                     "labels": {"host": host, "oid": oid}, "unit": "ms"},
                ],
                config_data=None,
                status="ok",
                reachable=True,
                error_message=None,
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
                rtt_ms=rtt_ms,
                response_time_ms=rtt_ms,
            )

        except Exception as exc:
            rtt_ms = round((time.monotonic() - snmp_start) * 1000, 2)
            execution_ms = int((time.monotonic() - poll_start) * 1000)

            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[
                    {"name": "snmp_reachable", "value": 0.0,
                     "labels": {"host": host, "oid": oid}},
                    {"name": "snmp_rtt_ms", "value": 0.0,
                     "labels": {"host": host, "oid": oid}, "unit": "ms"},
                ],
                config_data=None,
                status="critical",
                reachable=False,
                error_message=f"SNMP unreachable — {host}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
                error_category="device",
                rtt_ms=0.0,
                response_time_ms=0.0,
            )
